title: Mass air flow circuit malfunction
description: The ECU sees an electrical or plausibility fault in the MAF circuit.
probable_causes: EGR/boost deviation affecting air mass plausibility
step_by_step_fix: Inspect air filter and intake ducting for leaks or poor sealing.
Check MAF connector pins and wiring for corrosion or damage.
Compare MAF live data at idle and under load with expected values.
Inspect EGR and boost system if measured air mass is implausible.
Clean with proper MAF cleaner or replace only after confirming readings remain incorrect.
safety_warning: Keep hands and tools away from belts and fans while testing with the engine running.

title: Mass air flow circuit low input
description: The MAF signal is lower than expected or missing.
probable_causes: ECU plausibility issue caused by air leak
step_by_step_fix: Verify the MAF is plugged in and the connector latch is secure.
Check sensor power, ground, and signal with a multimeter or scope.
Inspect intake tract for restriction or collapsed hose.
Check live data for fixed or zero air mass value.
Replace the MAF if power/ground are correct and output remains low.
safety_warning: Do not probe connectors in a way that spreads terminals or creates shorts.

title: Intake air temperature sensor high input
description: The intake air temperature signal indicates an implausibly cold reading or open circuit.
step_by_step_fix: Read IAT live data with key on and compare to ambient temperature.
Inspect connector and wiring at the MAF/IAT sensor.
Check reference voltage and ground.
Repair wiring or replace the integrated sensor if readings remain implausible.
Clear code and confirm IAT tracks ambient/engine bay temperature.
safety_warning: Turn ignition off before unplugging engine sensors.

title: System too lean
description: Fuel trims indicate the engine is running lean on bank 1.
step_by_step_fix: Check short and long fuel trims at idle and under load.
Smoke-test intake/vacuum system for leaks.
Inspect MAF readings and intake boot condition.
Check fuel pressure and delivery if trims worsen under load.
Repair leak or sensor/fuel issue, then verify trims return near normal.
safety_warning: Fuel pressure testing involves flammable fuel. Work in a ventilated area away from ignition sources.

title: System too rich
description: Fuel trims indicate the engine is adding less fuel than normal because mixture is rich.
step_by_step_fix: Review fuel trims and oxygen sensor activity.
Inspect MAF readings and air filter condition.
Check fuel pressure against spec.
Test evap purge valve for leakage at idle.
Check injectors for leakage if rich condition persists.
safety_warning: Avoid open flames and sparks when testing fuel system components.

title: Injector circuit cylinder 1
description: The injector control circuit for cylinder 1 is open, shorted, or electrically implausible.
step_by_step_fix: Inspect injector connector and harness routing.
Check injector resistance and compare with other cylinders.
Use a noid/scope test if suitable for the injector type.
Swap components only if safe and supported by procedure.
Repair wiring or replace injector, then code/adapt injector if required.
safety_warning: Diesel common-rail injection can be extremely high pressure. Do not loosen fuel lines on a running engine.

title: Injector circuit cylinder 2
description: The injector control circuit for cylinder 2 is open, shorted, or electrically implausible.
step_by_step_fix: Inspect injector connector and harness routing.
Check injector resistance and compare with other cylinders.
Use a noid/scope test if suitable for the injector type.
Repair wiring or replace injector if the circuit fault is confirmed.
Code/adapt injector if required by the vehicle.
safety_warning: High-pressure diesel fuel can penetrate skin. Follow manufacturer depressurization procedures.

title: Injector circuit cylinder 3
description: The injector control circuit for cylinder 3 is open, shorted, or electrically implausible.
step_by_step_fix: Inspect injector connector and harness routing.
Check injector resistance and compare with other cylinders.
Check continuity between injector and ECU if needed.
Repair wiring or replace injector after confirmation.
Clear codes and confirm cylinder contribution returns to normal.
safety_warning: Do not disconnect injectors with ignition on unless the service procedure allows it.

title: Injector circuit cylinder 4
description: The injector control circuit for cylinder 4 is open, shorted, or electrically implausible.
step_by_step_fix: Inspect injector connector and harness routing.
Check injector resistance and compare with other cylinders.
Check for oil or fuel contamination in connectors.
Repair wiring or replace injector after confirmation.
Perform injector coding/adaptation if required.
safety_warning: Use eye protection around fuel systems and follow depressurization procedures.

title: Turbocharger overboost
description: Boost pressure exceeded the ECU target or safety threshold.
step_by_step_fix: Compare requested and actual boost during a controlled road test.
Inspect vacuum/pressure control lines and turbo actuator movement.
Check MAP sensor readings key-on and under load.
Inspect turbo vane mechanism for sticking if accessible.
Repair control fault before replacing the turbo.
safety_warning: Avoid repeated full-load tests while overboost is present; it can damage the turbo or engine.

title: EGR flow malfunction
description: The ECU detected an EGR flow or control problem.
step_by_step_fix: Read EGR commanded/actual position and air mass live data.
Inspect EGR connector and actuator operation.
Check intake/EGR passages for carbon restriction.
Verify no boost or MAF fault is causing EGR plausibility issues.
Clean or replace EGR components as confirmed by testing.
safety_warning: Let EGR/exhaust parts cool before removal; soot is irritating, use gloves and mask.

title: EGR flow excessive
description: The ECU detected more EGR flow than expected.
step_by_step_fix: Check EGR actual position at idle and during snap throttle.
Inspect EGR for sticking or carbon preventing closure.
Compare MAF readings when EGR is commanded closed.
Check wiring and position feedback.
Clean or replace EGR valve if it cannot close correctly.
safety_warning: Avoid driving if the engine stalls or loses power in traffic.

title: EGR range/performance
description: The EGR position or flow response is outside expected limits.
step_by_step_fix: Command EGR movement with a scan tool if supported.
Inspect live data for commanded vs actual EGR position.
Check connector pins and wiring.
Inspect and clean EGR valve/cooler passages.
Replace the valve/actuator if movement or feedback is faulty.
safety_warning: Disconnect battery where required before removing electrical actuators.

title: Exhaust pressure sensor range/performance
description: The exhaust backpressure signal is implausible or outside expected range.
step_by_step_fix: Check sensor reading key-on/engine-off for plausibility.
Inspect pressure tube/hoses for blockage or leaks.
Check wiring, power, ground, and signal.
Compare exhaust pressure under load to expected values.
Replace sensor or clear blockage before condemning DPF.
safety_warning: Exhaust pressure tubes and sensors may be very hot after running.

title: Cooling fan control circuit
description: The ECU detected a fault in fan control circuit operation.
symptoms: Fan not running or runs constantly
step_by_step_fix: Check coolant temperature and fan command with scanner.
Inspect fuses, relays, and fan module supply/ground.
Command fan operation with diagnostic tool if supported.
Check wiring and connector for heat damage.
Replace fan/module only after power and command checks pass.
safety_warning: Cooling fans can start unexpectedly. Keep fingers and tools clear whenever ignition is on.

title: Exhaust gas temperature sensor circuit
description: An exhaust gas temperature sensor signal is implausible or circuit is faulty.
step_by_step_fix: Identify which EGT sensor position the code refers to using scanner data.
Check sensor temperature reading cold; it should be near ambient.
Inspect wiring near exhaust for melting or chafing.
Measure sensor resistance/voltage according to service info.
Replace the faulty sensor and verify regeneration readiness.
safety_warning: EGT sensors are mounted in very hot exhaust parts. Let the exhaust cool fully.

title: Glow plug control module circuit
description: The glow plug controller or its supply/control circuit is faulty.
probable_causes: Failed glow plug causing module fault
step_by_step_fix: Check battery voltage and glow plug module power/ground.
Scan for cylinder-specific glow plug codes.
Measure glow plug resistance if accessible.
Inspect wiring from module to glow plugs.
Replace failed plugs/module as confirmed and clear codes.
safety_warning: Glow plugs can seize in the cylinder head. Remove only on a warm engine and with proper technique.

title: Glow plug cylinder 1 circuit
description: Cylinder 1 glow plug circuit has an electrical fault.
step_by_step_fix: Measure cylinder 1 glow plug resistance and compare with others.
Inspect wiring and connector at glow plug/module.
Check module output if the plug tests good.
Replace failed glow plug or repair wiring.
Clear codes and retest cold start.
safety_warning: Do not force a seized glow plug; broken tips can require cylinder head repair.

title: Glow plug cylinder 2 circuit
description: Cylinder 2 glow plug circuit has an electrical fault.
step_by_step_fix: Measure cylinder 2 glow plug resistance and compare with others.
Inspect harness and connector condition.
Check module output if the plug tests good.
Replace failed glow plug or repair wiring.
Clear codes and retest.
safety_warning: Use correct torque and removal technique for glow plugs.

title: Glow plug cylinder 3 circuit
description: Cylinder 3 glow plug circuit has an electrical fault.
step_by_step_fix: Measure cylinder 3 glow plug resistance and compare with others.
Inspect wiring and module connector.
Check module output if the plug tests good.
Replace failed part and clear codes.
Verify cold-start performance.
safety_warning: Glow plug extraction can damage the cylinder head if rushed.

title: Glow plug cylinder 4 circuit
description: Cylinder 4 glow plug circuit has an electrical fault.
step_by_step_fix: Measure cylinder 4 glow plug resistance and compare with others.
Inspect wiring and connector condition.
Check module output if needed.
Replace failed plug/module or repair wiring.
Clear codes and retest cold.
safety_warning: Let the engine cool enough to work safely, but follow glow plug removal best practices.

title: DPF efficiency below threshold
description: The diesel particulate filter is not trapping soot as expected or pressure/soot model is implausible.
step_by_step_fix: Check DPF pressure sensor readings key-on and at idle.
Inspect pressure hoses and exhaust for leaks.
Check soot/ash load values in diagnostic tool.
Verify no sensor faults are skewing efficiency calculation.
Replace or professionally clean/inspect DPF if substrate or loading is confirmed.
safety_warning: DPF and exhaust components can reach extreme temperatures during regeneration.

title: Intake manifold runner position sensor range/performance
description: The intake runner/swirl flap position signal is outside expected range.
step_by_step_fix: Command runner actuator with scan tool if supported.
Observe linkage movement and check for binding.
Inspect intake for heavy carbon/oil deposits.
Check actuator connector and position feedback.
Repair actuator/linkage or replace manifold assembly if worn.
safety_warning: Prevent debris entering intake ports during manifold work.

title: Exhaust gas temperature sensor circuit high
description: An EGT sensor signal is too high or open-circuit depending on sensor strategy.
step_by_step_fix: Identify the sensor position from manufacturer data.
Compare cold EGT readings with ambient temperature.
Inspect wiring near exhaust heat zones.
Measure sensor resistance/voltage if specified.
Replace the faulty sensor and confirm aftertreatment monitors run.
safety_warning: Let exhaust cool completely before touching EGT sensors.

title: Exhaust gas temperature sensor range/performance
description: The EGT sensor signal is plausible electrically but not behaving as expected.
step_by_step_fix: Compare all EGT sensor values cold; they should be similar.
Monitor EGT rise during warm-up and regeneration request.
Inspect harness and connector for heat damage.
Check for exhaust leaks near sensor.
Replace the sensor if it is slow/biased compared with others.
safety_warning: Do not perform regeneration tests in enclosed spaces.

title: NOx sensor circuit bank 1
description: The NOx sensor module or circuit has an electrical/communication fault.
step_by_step_fix: Identify upstream/downstream NOx sensor from scan data.
Check wiring and connector for heat/water damage.
Verify sensor module power/ground and communication.
Check for related AdBlue/SCR codes before replacement.
Replace/carry out adaptation or software procedure as required.
safety_warning: Exhaust-mounted NOx sensors get very hot; follow SCR service procedures.

title: NOx sensor range/performance
description: NOx sensor output is implausible for current operating conditions.
step_by_step_fix: Check for SCR/AdBlue quality, pressure, heater, or dosing codes.
Inspect exhaust leaks before NOx sensors.
Monitor NOx readings before/after SCR catalyst.
Verify wiring and sensor module communication.
Replace/adapt NOx sensor if readings remain implausible after related faults are fixed.
safety_warning: AdBlue/SCR diagnosis may involve hot exhaust and chemical handling; wear gloves and eye protection.

title: Turbocharger boost system performance
description: Overall turbo/boost system performance is outside expected range.
step_by_step_fix: Smoke-test or pressure-test charge-air system.
Compare requested and actual boost during a road test.
Inspect actuator movement and vacuum/pressure control.
Check MAP/boost sensor plausibility.
Evaluate DPF pressure if boost fault appears with exhaust restriction.
safety_warning: Secure the vehicle and avoid unsafe full-load road tests.

title: DPF ash accumulation
description: The ECU estimates excessive ash accumulation in the DPF.
step_by_step_fix: Check soot and ash load values with a capable scanner.
Confirm differential pressure readings are plausible.
Check engine oil consumption and correct low-ash oil specification.
Do not force regeneration if ash load is beyond limit.
Remove and professionally clean or replace DPF if ash load is confirmed high.
safety_warning: Forced regeneration will not remove ash and can overheat an overloaded DPF.

title: DPF differential pressure sensor circuit
description: The differential pressure sensor circuit has an electrical fault.
step_by_step_fix: Check sensor power, ground, and signal.
Inspect connector and wiring near hot exhaust areas.
Verify pressure hoses are intact and not blocked.
Read pressure key-on/engine-off; it should be near zero.
Replace sensor or repair wiring as confirmed.
safety_warning: Avoid touching exhaust/DPF components until fully cool.

title: DPF differential pressure sensor low
description: The DPF pressure sensor signal is lower than expected.
step_by_step_fix: Check pressure sensor live data key-on and running.
Inspect both pressure hoses for leaks/disconnection.
Check sensor wiring for short to ground.
Verify sensor supply and ground.
Repair hose/wiring or replace sensor after confirmation.
safety_warning: Exhaust pressure hoses may contain soot; wear gloves and eye protection.

title: DPF differential pressure sensor high
description: The DPF pressure sensor signal is higher than expected.
step_by_step_fix: Read pressure key-on/engine-off; high reading indicates sensor/wiring issue.
Inspect pressure hoses for blockage or incorrect routing.
Check wiring for short to voltage.
Measure differential pressure under idle/raised RPM.
Repair sensor/hose issue before deciding DPF is blocked.
safety_warning: Do not perform forced regeneration until sensor plausibility is confirmed.

title: Transmission control system malfunction
description: The engine ECU is reporting that the transmission control module has stored a fault.
probable_causes: Conductor plate/speed sensor fault on some Mercedes transmissions
step_by_step_fix: Scan the transmission control module, not only engine OBD.
Record exact transmission-specific codes and freeze-frame data.
Check fluid level/condition if serviceable by procedure.
Inspect transmission connector sleeve for oil ingress where applicable.
Repair based on TCM codes; P0700 alone is only a pointer.
safety_warning: Support vehicle securely if checking transmission underside; hot fluid can burn.

title: Input/turbine speed sensor circuit
description: Transmission input speed signal is missing or implausible.
probable_causes: Internal transmission speed sensor/conductor plate fault
step_by_step_fix: Scan TCM for manufacturer-specific details.
Check live data for input speed while selecting gears.
Inspect external connector for oil ingress/corrosion.
Check fluid condition if serviceable.
Repair conductor plate/sensor or wiring according to transmission type.
safety_warning: Transmission work may require lifting the vehicle; use proper stands and fluid handling.

title: Output speed sensor circuit
description: Transmission output speed signal is missing or implausible.
step_by_step_fix: Read TCM-specific codes and live output speed data.
Inspect transmission connector for fluid ingress.
Check wiring continuity if external fault suspected.
Evaluate conductor plate/speed sensor assembly if common to transmission type.
Clear adaptations/codes and road-test after repair.
safety_warning: Hot ATF can burn; follow proper fill-level temperature procedure.

title: Lost communication with ECM/PCM
description: A control module cannot communicate with the engine control module.
step_by_step_fix: Check battery voltage and charging system first.
Scan all modules and note which modules cannot communicate.
Check ECU fuses, relays, power, and grounds.
Inspect CAN wiring/connectors for water ingress or damage.
Repair network/power issue before replacing modules.
safety_warning: Disconnect battery according to manufacturer procedure before unplugging major control modules.

title: Lost communication with ABS/ESP control module
description: A module cannot communicate with the ABS/ESP control module.
step_by_step_fix: Check battery/charging voltage and all ABS fuses.
Scan ABS/ESP module directly if possible.
Inspect ABS module connector and wiring for water or corrosion.
Check CAN high/low resistance and shorts if communication is lost.
Repair power/network issue before replacing ABS module.
safety_warning: Brake/ABS faults affect vehicle safety. Do not drive normally until repaired.
